title: armeria arenaria subsp. confusa
intro: Armeria arenaria subsp. confusa, also known as the thrift or sea pink, is a plant species within the armeria genus that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Armeria arenaria subsp. confusa include its ability to soothe sore throats, calm digestive issues, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Armeria arenaria subsp. confusa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
